text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic protac rectopexy, utilizing minimally invasive techniques to secure the rectum in place.
The surgeon performs total mesorectal excision (TME) mobilization of the rectum, carefully dissecting down to the pelvic floor.
The surgeon divides the peritoneum bilaterally, creating a visible separation on both sides of the pelvic cavity.
The surgeon demonstrates the mobility of the rectum on the right side, indicating successful dissection and mobilization in preparation for rectopexy.
The surgeon completes the division of the peritoneum on the patient's left side, using laparoscopic instruments to carefully separate the tissue.
The surgeon releases the peritoneal attachments of the sigmoid colon, visibly straightening the sigmoid as part of the laparoscopic rectopexy procedure.
The rectum is visibly mobile, indicating successful mobilization within the pelvic cavity.
The surgeon uses protacks to secure the peritoneum to the sacral promontory, placing three protacks on each side bilaterally.
The surgeon secures the rectum to the sacral promontory, visibly resolving the prolapse as the rectum is stabilized in its new position.
